title: Vattal Kurishu Palli
tagline: Vattal Kurishu Palli — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Vattal Kurisu Pally in Maikavu is one of Kerala's oldest Syriac Orthodox pilgrim centers.
overview.description: Vattal Kurisu Pally in Maikavu is one of Kerala's oldest Syriac Orthodox pilgrim centers. The patron saints of this parish are Saint George and Parumala Thirumeni. Situated by the side of coconut palms and a wide stretch of arecanut fields, at a village called Maikavu which is about 40 km from Kozhikode, this church is the refuge of thousands of people worldwide who seek the intercession of St. George.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Orthodox Church, Maikavu
topPlaces.description: St. Mary's Orthodox Church is one of the old churches in Malabar Diocese of the Malankara Orthodox Church.

topPlaces.name: Maikave
topPlaces.description: Maikave is a village in the Kozhikode district of Northern Kerala (Malabar), on average 4.1m above sea level and near a river. It is located near Thamarassery (about 7 km away).

topPlaces.name: Kedavoor
topPlaces.description: Kedavoor is a village in Kozhikode district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Kedavur
topPlaces.description: Kedavur is a village in Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thiruvambady
topPlaces.description: Thiruvambady is a major town in Kozhikode district in Kerala, India which lies near foothills of Wayanad. Thiruvambady Assembly constituency is a Legislative Assembly Constituency of Kerala, situated 35 kilometres (22 mi) from west of Kozhikode.

topPlaces.name: Kattippara
topPlaces.description: Kattippara (Hard Rock in Malayalam) often called as Kattipara, is a Panchayat in Kozhikode district, Kerala. It is located about 10 km from Thamarassery town.

topPlaces.name: Technical HSS Thamarassery
topPlaces.description: IHRD Thamarassery, also called Technical HSS Thamarassery, is an Indian Technical Higher Secondary School established in 2004. Located in Korangad on the Thamarassery-Balussery State Highway route in Kozhikode district, it is approximately 2 kilometers away from Thamarassery Town.

topPlaces.name: Thamarassery
topPlaces.description: Thamarassery, formerly known as Thazhmalachery, is one of the taluks and a major hill town in the Kozhikode district of Kerala, India, 30 km north-east of Kozhikode city and 29 km east of Koyilandy. The town lies on National highway 766.

items.note: St. Mary's Orthodox Church is one of the old churches in Malabar Diocese of the Malankara Orthodox Church.
Maikave is a village in the Kozhikode district of Northern Kerala (Malabar), on average 4.1m above sea level and near a river. It is located near Thamarassery (about 7 km away).
Kedavoor is a village in Kozhikode district in the Indian state of Kerala.
Kedavur is a village in Kozhikode district in the state of Kerala, India.

Thiruvambady is a major town in Kozhikode district in Kerala, India which lies near foothills of Wayanad. Thiruvambady Assembly constituency is a Legislative Assembly Constituency of Kerala, situated 35 kilometres (22 mi) from west of Kozhikode.
Kattippara (Hard Rock in Malayalam) often called as Kattipara, is a Panchayat in Kozhikode district, Kerala. It is located about 10 km from Thamarassery town.

IHRD Thamarassery, also called Technical HSS Thamarassery, is an Indian Technical Higher Secondary School established in 2004. Located in Korangad on the Thamarassery-Balussery State Highway route in Kozhikode district, it is approximately 2 kilometers away from Thamarassery Town.

Thamarassery, formerly known as Thazhmalachery, is one of the taluks and a major hill town in the Kozhikode district of Kerala, India, 30 km north-east of Kozhikode city and 29 km east of Koyilandy. The town lies on National highway 766.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vattal Kurishu Palli?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vattal Kurishu Palli is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vattal Kurishu Palli?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Calicut International Airport, Kozhikode (30 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (116 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vattal Kurishu Palli?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vattal Kurishu Palli at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vattal Kurishu Palli?
faq.a: Stays in Vattal Kurishu Palli start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vattal Kurishu Palli?
faq.a: The top attractions are St. Mary's Orthodox Church, Maikavu, Maikave, Kedavoor, Kedavur, Thiruvambady.
